one_line_reason: Right-shaped deal — a web app the team can fully own and scale, so operability scores high — but everything that would make it a business (users, retention, revenue, transferable IP) is undisclosed and unverifiable; completeness ~38% is too low to trust any tier, so v2 returns INSUFFICIENT DATA rather than the confident MARGINAL v1 implied.

operability_upside.note: A Pomodoro/focus web app is dead-center in the team's build-and-scale wheelhouse — they can own it end-to-end, modernize, add AI, and scale. Operability is the one thing genuinely verified here.

pmf_retention.note: No users, MAU, or retention disclosed and zero public footprint — genuine unknown, not evidence of bad PMF; scored neutral. (Focus timers are a notoriously low-retention category, but nothing is verified.)

unit_economics.note: No revenue/MRR/margins disclosed at a $1.5K price — almost certainly pre-revenue but unverified; scored neutral-unknown rather than assuming zero.

moat_defensibility.note: Red-ocean category (Pomofocus, Forest, Session, Flow, Todoist, Notion) is verified saturation, but this product's specific defensibility is unknown; neutral with low confidence.

growth_market.note: Huge but heavily saturated productivity/focus TAM; an AI 'focus coach' angle exists but is crowded. Market size known, this app's growth path unknown.

tech_ip.note: It's a web app the team could own, but pomocalm.com returned an empty shell on fetch and the codebase/stack/ownership is unverified (could be a paid template/boilerplate).

transfer_key_person.note: No distribution or audience exists to depend on a key person, but equally nothing verified transfers (users, domain reputation). Genuine unknown, neutral.

deal_terms.note: $1,500 is cheap in absolute terms but poor value for a team that can rebuild a Pomodoro app in a weekend unless real engaged users convey; terms/what-conveys unverified.

seller_adverse_selection.note: 'Offloading distracting projects to focus on e-commerce' reads as an abandoned hobby, and withholding all metrics is an adverse-selection signal — evidence-based caution, scored neutral.

key_unknowns: Are there ANY real, engaged users or any revenue? This is the whole deal — with none you're buying rebuildable code. Swings the verdict from Pass to Promising.
Retention: do users return after a few weeks? Pomodoro/focus tools churn hard; decisive for whether a business exists.
Is there a functioning app at all (fetch returned an empty shell) and is 100% of the code owned + transferable (not a license-limited template/boilerplate)?
Any acquisition channel with traction, and is the #1 growth lever product-led/organic vs paid/SEO/founder-audience? Zero public footprint suggests no distribution exists.
Ongoing costs/liabilities (hosting, APIs, auth, payments) and whether any paying user or the domain reputation actually transfers.
seller_questions: The decisive one: current registered users, MAU/WAU, and any revenue/MRR — screen-shared from analytics + Stripe/billing. (PMF + unit economics + revenue verification)
Retention: of users who sign up, what share are still active at 4 weeks? (retention)
Where do users come from and how many visits/month — organic, Product Hunt, ads, or none? (growth lever + channel; there's no public footprint, so this reveals whether any distribution exists)

Exactly what transfers: repo/codebase, domain, and the user database — and is 100% of the code owned by you (no license-limited template/boilerplate)? What's the stack/hosting? (tech + IP transferability)
How long has it been live, is there any monetization today, and why sell at $1,500? (adverse selection)
Monthly running costs and any third-party dependencies (auth, payments, APIs)? (economics)
inputs_provided: Listing: 'web-app for organizing your workload and prompting you to work in a productive digital environment' [seller-stated]
Asking price $1,500, sale via Reddit DM [seller-stated]
Reason for sale: offloading distracting projects to focus on e-commerce [seller-stated -> abandonment signal]
No revenue, traffic, or user numbers disclosed [OBSERVED — notable omission]
pomocalm.com returned an empty shell on fetch; not verified as a functioning app [OBSERVED]
Public search for 'pomocalm': zero footprint — no Product Hunt, reviews, or mentions [OBSERVED -> no traction signal]
scorecard_markdown:
## Acquisition Scorecard (v2) — pomocalm.com

**Verdict:** INSUFFICIENT DATA — merit reads MARGINAL (49/100) but completeness ~38% is too low to trust. Gather metrics, then re-score. Asking $1,500 (Reddit DM), no financing.

Right-shaped but nearly unverifiable. Operability is the only thing genuinely known; every business-defining dimension (users, retention, revenue, transferable IP) is undisclosed, so v2 refuses a confident tier.

### Dimensions
- operability_upside 5/5 (high) — Pomodoro/focus web app is dead-center in the team's wheelhouse; ownable and scalable.
- pmf_retention 2/5 (unknown) — no users/retention disclosed, zero footprint; neutral-unknown, not evidence of bad PMF.
- unit_economics 2/5 (unknown) — no revenue/margins at a $1.5K price; likely pre-revenue but unverified.
- moat_defensibility 2/5 (low) — red-ocean category is verified, this app's defensibility is not.
- growth_market 2/5 (med) — large but saturated TAM; crowded AI-focus-coach angle.
- tech_ip 2/5 (low) — site returned an empty shell; codebase/stack/ownership unverified (possible template).
- transfer_key_person 2/5 (low) — no audience to depend on, but nothing verified conveys either.
- deal_terms 2/5 (low) — $1,500 cheap absolute, poor value vs a weekend rebuild unless real users convey.
- seller_adverse_selection 2/5 (med) — 'offloading to focus on e-commerce' + withheld metrics = abandonment/adverse-selection signal.

### Gates
- operable: unknown (fetch returned an empty shell)
- growth_lever_fits: unknown (no channel identified)
- ip_transferable: unknown (could be a template)
- value_transfers: unknown (no distribution to survive exit)
- not_fragile: unknown
- revenue_evidence: unknown (all metrics withheld)

### Key unknowns
Any real engaged users/revenue; 4-week retention; whether a functioning, fully-owned app exists; any traction channel; running costs and what actually transfers.
